title: pedicularis sudetica subsp. interior
intro: Pedicularis sudetica subsp. interior is a subspecies of the Pedicularis sudetica plant, commonly known as the lousewort or mountain lousewort, a flowering plant in the balm family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and treating wounds, due to its rich content of iridoid glycosides and other bioactive compounds. Pedicularis sudetica subsp. interior can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in folk medicine, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
